description: RMD planning call with Margaret Johnson, age 74, IRA at Schwab

transcript_segments.text: Hi Margaret, thanks for hopping on. I wanted to talk through your required minimum distribution for this year.
So you turned 74 in February, which means you're in your second RMD year — the first one was last year after you hit 73.
Based on your December 31st IRA balance of about 640,000 dollars, this year's RMD comes out to roughly 25,000 dollars.
That's using the Uniform Lifetime Table divisor of 25.5 for age 74. The IRS deadline is December 31st.
Okay. Last year I waited until December and it was kind of stressful. Can we set it up earlier this year?
Absolutely. I'd recommend we schedule the distribution for early October — gives us a buffer if Schwab needs any paperwork.
I'll send you the distribution request form this week. You'll need to choose a withholding percentage — most clients pick 10 to 15 percent federal.
Ten percent sounds right. Same as last year. Should I do anything about state tax withholding?
California doesn't require withholding on IRA distributions but you can opt in. We can handle that when you sign the form.
One more thing — have you thought about doing a qualified charitable distribution? You mentioned the church fund drive last time.
Oh right, the QCD. Yes, I'd like to send 5,000 directly to the church. That counts toward the RMD, right?
Exactly — up to 108,000 this year can go direct to charity and it comes out of your RMD requirement, tax-free.
So we'll do 5,000 QCD to the church, and 20,000 to your checking account in early October.
Perfect. I'll send the paperwork today and we'll touch base in September to confirm everything.

must_mention.fact: Qualified Charitable Distribution of $5,000 to church

prohibited.reason: Roth conversions were not discussed on this call.
Margaret is in her second RMD year, not her first.
Portfolio rebalancing was not discussed — this is an RMD logistics call.

expected_citations.for_fact: Qualified Charitable Distribution of $5,000 to church

rubric.check: Action items owner is the advisor, not Margaret (she signs forms, advisor sends them)
TLDR mentions RMD planning, not generic 'portfolio review'
QCD amount cited as $5,000 — not conflated with the $108,000 annual cap
